data.title: TKO signs over majority control to Endeavor to bolster portfolio with PBR, On Location, and IMG assets (update)
Anglo American keeps copper, diamond output guidance; sees progress on copper pivot
data.description:
Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) +2.9% in London trading Thursday after maintaining production targets for key metals and issuing upbeat remarks on the progress on its planned restructuring that would shed of most of its assets.

The first unit to be carved out from the group is its steelmaking coal division, and Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said the final round of bidders is in place and that it expects to announce a sale agreement in the coming months.

Analysts at Jefferies have said the coal unit was worth ~$4.5B before a fire incident in June at the Grosvenor site in Australia; mining operations have since been suspended at the mine, which accounts for ~30% of the unit's value.

Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said recent imagery shows limited damage at Grosvenor's underground area, but the company's Q3 total steelmaking coal output fell 6% due to the mine's suspension.

Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said Q3 copper output fell 13% Y/Y, largely due to the planed closure of a processing plant at its Los Bronces mine in Chile, while rough diamond production sank 25% following cuts in response to prolonged weak demand.

The company said the De Beers diamond unit plans to assess options for further production cuts, as the diamond market remains challenging.

Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) raised its full-year production guidance for nickel and platinum metals slightly, while it kept its outlook for all other assets; the miner still expects to produce 730K-790K tons of copper and 23M-26M carats of rough diamonds this year.

data.title: Keurig Dr Pepper makes an energizing M&A move
data.description:
Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) traded 5.4% lower on Thursday after missing revenue estimates with its Q3 earnings report. However, the company's also announced an M&A play that has some analysts intrigued.

The beverage company announced the acquisition of Ghost Energy for $990 million. KPD will have an initial stake of 60% stake in Ghost Energy, with the remaining 40% in 2028 at pre-negotiated valuation parameters.

Ghost Energy, launched in 2020, is a relatively new player in the energy drink market. The brand was created as an extension of the Ghost lifestyle sports nutrition line, founded in 2016 by Dan Lourenco and Ryan Hughes. Ghost Energy's inception came through a partnership with Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD), after the company noticed customers were purchasing energy drinks alongside other Ghost products at GNC stores. The brand then expanded to national distribution deals with major chains like Kroger (KR), 7-Eleven, and Publix by 2022. Ghost Energy says it differentiates itself through transparency, noting that it is the first full-disclosure energy drink to clearly list active ingredients on the nutrition facts panel.

Morgan Stanley analyst Dara Mohsenian said the Ghost energy deal structure looks favorable, with Ghost management incentivized to drive continued momentum. "The deal makes strategic sense, giving KDP another solid brand (with distribution expansion potential) in the high-growth energy category (albeit with near-term macro pressure) in its energy portfolio next to its recent C-4 distribution agreement, and relative to a weaker LT track record in energy before these brands," he added. Based on a net $850M purchase price after $140M in tax benefits, the deal was noted to be occurring at ~3X 2024 revenues, although if the $250M in distributor transition payments were added in, the multiple would rise by a half multiple point. Notably, Ghost sales grew 11.9% year-over-year in the latest 12 weeks of scanner data and 19.3% in the last 52 weeks, despite broad category weakness. In terms of potential distribution expansion, Ghost's all commodity volume (a distribution measure) is 73% vs. Red Bull at 95%, Monster Beverage (MNST) at 96%, Celsius Holdings (CELH) at 94%, and C4 at 82%.

data.title: Converge Technology slumps after company lowers Q3 guidance
data.description:
Converge Technology Solutions (OTCQX:CTSDF) dropped over 14% after the IT & cloud solutions provider said it expects lower-than-anticipated results for the third quarter of 2024.

Based on preliminary estimates, the company now expects revenue to be in the range of C$630M to C$631M, down from previous guidance of C$636M to C$658M, while adjusted EBITDA is seen between C$31.8M to C$32.3M vs. C$43M to C$47M previously.

That would imply a year-on-year decline in quarterly revenue from C$710.1M recorder in the third quarter of 2023.

“Q3 revenue was impacted by adverse macroeconomic conditions as certain customers delayed project spend as timelines shifted into the fourth quarter of 2024 or potentially the next fiscal year. Compared to Q3-2023, Q3-2024 revenue declined due to lower demand in North America for data center, networking, and storage solutions whereas Q3-2023 included a period of abnormally strong backlog fulfillment,” the company said in a statement.

Converge (OTCQX:CTSDF) will release its complete financial results for the third quarter along with an updated financial outlook for the fiscal year on November 12, 2024.

data.title: LinkedIn Ireland receives $335M fine from Data Protection Commission
data.description:
Microsoft-owned (NASDAQ:MSFT) LinkedIn received a €310M, or $335M, fine from the Irish Data Protection Commission, or DPC, related to digital advertising efforts in 2018, but LinkedIn believes they have complied with data regulations.

"This inquiry examined the lawfulness, fairness and transparency of the processing of the personal data of users of the LinkedIn platform for the purposes of behavioral analysis and targeted advertising," the Irish DPC said. "The personal data in question encompassed data provided directly to LinkedIn by its members (first-party data) and data obtained via its third-party partners relating to its members (third-party data)."

The DPC found that LinkedIn did not properly process the data of LinkedIn users for advertising purposes in a fair and transparent manner.

"The lawfulness of processing is a fundamental aspect of data protection law and the processing of personal data without an appropriate legal basis is a clear and serious violation of a data subjects' fundamental right to data protection," said DPC Deputy Commissioner Graham Doyle.

LinkedIn believes they have complied with the DPC's data regulations.

"Today, the Irish Data Protection Commission reached a final decision on claims from 2018 about some of our digital advertising efforts in the EU," said a LinkedIn spokesperson to Seeking Alpha. "While we believe we have been in compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation, we are working to ensure our ad practices meet this decision by the IDPC's deadline."

The fine was less than Microsoft had anticipated in a 10-K disclosure, as the tech titan had expected it to total about $425M.

Microsoft shares were flat on Thursday.
